data.title: Senators Propose Bill to Protect Blockchain Developers' Rights
data.description:
Senators Cynthia Lummis and Ron Wyden introduce bill to protect blockchain developers from being classified as money transmitters.
source: https://coincu.com/news/senators-bill-blockchain-developers-rights/

data.title: U.S. Inflation Predicted to Rise in Late 2025 and Early 2026
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, China International Capital Corporation (CICC) forecasts that U.S. inflation will experience compensatory increases in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) data for December 2025, January 2026, and April 2026.

If U.S. inflation remains strong in the near term, it could prompt the Federal Reserve to slow down its rate cuts, leading to a marginal tightening of global liquidity. This scenario may increase the uncertainty of major asset classes both domestically and internationally. CICC advises increasing allocations in commodities to hedge against risks and suggests buying stocks, gold, and U.S. Treasury bonds during market pullbacks.

data.title: US stocks closed higher across the board, with the crypto sector seeing broad gains.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the close of trading on the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.17%, the S&P 500 rose 0.16%, and the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.26%. The crypto sector generally rose, with Coinbase up 0.91%, BitMine up 3.56%, and SharpLink up 2.40%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stocks and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: BlackRock's 2026 Global Outlook Highlights AI Investment and Economic Impacts
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on January 13, BlackRock released its 2026 global outlook report, emphasizing the significant scale of AI infrastructure investments. The report suggests that 'micro is macro,' indicating that these investments are substantial enough to influence the broader macroeconomic landscape, presenting challenges such as increased leverage and the illusion of diversification. BlackRock maintains a pro-risk stance, favoring U.S. equities, particularly those related to AI, and sees opportunities in active investments.

The report outlines three core investment themes:

Micro is macro: AI infrastructure is dominated by a few companies, with capital expenditures projected to reach $5-8 trillion between 2025 and 2030. This investment is expected to support U.S. economic growth in 2026, contributing three times the historical average, despite a cooling labor market. However, there is uncertainty about whether revenues will match expenditures and how much will return to tech giants. While AI may accelerate innovation, historical trends over the past 150 years suggest that major technological changes have not disrupted the U.S.'s long-term 2% growth trend. Nonetheless, a 'growth breakout' scenario is now conceivable.

Leveraging up: The initial massive investments by AI builders, coupled with delayed revenues, lead to increased system leverage. High government debt adds to this vulnerability. BlackRock favors private credit and infrastructure financing while tactically underweighting long-term government bonds, such as U.S. Treasuries, due to the adverse effects of high leverage and rising capital costs on long-term debt.

Diversification mirage: Under major trends, traditional diversification strategies may actually concentrate bets. Investors need to actively manage risk, maintain portfolio flexibility with a 'Plan B,' and seek unique returns from private markets and hedge funds.

The report also highlights that BlackRock views digital assets, particularly stablecoins, as foundational to payment and settlement systems, rather than merely speculative assets. Stablecoins are seen as 'digital dollar rails,' evolving from crypto-native tools to bridges connecting traditional finance with digital liquidity, expanding into areas like cross-border payments and settlements, especially in regions where traditional systems are slow, costly, or fragmented. The report suggests that crypto is integrating into mainstream finance, with stablecoins maturing as infrastructure supporting global liquidity flows and overlapping with traditional finance.

data.title: The Thai Prime Minister has ordered stricter regulations on gold trading and digital assets to combat illicit funds.
data.description: According to The Nation Thailand, Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul has officially launched a comprehensive campaign against gray and illicit financial flows, ordering stricter regulations on gold trading and digital assets. In the digital asset sector, the government will strictly enforce the Travel Rule, requiring the identification of senders and receivers in all wallet-to-wallet transfers to eliminate anonymity. Furthermore, Thailand will establish a national Data Bureau to centrally manage financial data, enabling regulators to track suspicious transactions in real time. Currently, the Thai government is instructing the Anti-Money Laundering Office (AMLO) to lower the reporting threshold for gold transactions from 2 million baht and is studying the possibility of imposing a special business tax on online gold trading.

data.title: Thailand Initiates Crackdown on Illicit Funds and Tightens Digital Asset Regulations
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on January 13, Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul has launched a comprehensive campaign against 'grey funds' and illegal financial flows, ordering stricter regulations on gold trading and digital assets. In the digital asset sector, the government will rigorously enforce the 'Travel Rule,' mandating identification of both sender and receiver in all wallet-to-wallet transfers to eliminate anonymity.

Additionally, Thailand plans to establish a National Data Bureau to centralize financial data management, enabling regulatory bodies to track suspicious transactions in real-time.

data.title: Nigeria Implements New Tax Law to Track Cryptocurrency Transactions
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on January 13, the Nigerian government introduced a new tax law that links cryptocurrency transactions to real identities using Tax Identification Numbers (TIN) and National Identification Numbers (NIN). This initiative aims to make crypto transactions traceable and integrate them into the tax reporting system without needing to break the blockchain itself.

Virtual Asset Service Providers (VASPs) are now required to collect and report customer information, including TIN/NIN, names, and addresses. They must also submit monthly transaction data to tax authorities and report large or suspicious transactions to law enforcement agencies.

data.title: Binance: Booster Earn receipt tokens will be credited to Alpha balance points.
data.description: According to official news, Binance Alpha Balance Points will be updated at 00:00 on January 13, 2026. All Binance Booster Earn receipt tokens, including tETH, miBNB, maUSDT, zkUSDT, bwUSDT, and receipt tokens from subsequent Booster Earn events, will be credited to users' Binance Alpha Balance Points.

data.title: Former New York City Mayor Eric Adams launched NYC Token, which quickly surged and then fell back after opening.
data.description: Former New York City Mayor Eric Adams has launched a cryptocurrency called NYC Token. The project, co-founded by Adams and two other members, aims to fund non-profit organizations to combat anti-Semitism and anti-Americanism through the token's proceeds. Adams stated that the token will also be used to educate students about blockchain technology. Adams currently does not receive a salary and said he will disclose it publicly if he does in the future. The project's website currently only displays a slogan and a link to an X platform account. Current New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has stated he will not purchase the token.

data.title: pump.fun transferred another 148 million USDC and USDT to Kraken.
data.description: According to Ember's monitoring, one hour ago, pump.fun transferred another 148 million USDC and USDT obtained through the ICO sale of PUMP to Kraken. In the two months since November 15, 2024, pump.fun (4jtZw...ZqqE) has cumulatively transferred 753 million USDC and USDT obtained through the ICO sale of PUMP in June 2025 to Kraken.

data.title: CFTC forms innovation committee to help shape rules for crypto, AI
data.description:
The CFTC is looking to invite Tyler Winklevoss, Shayne Coplan, Kris Marszalek, and other crypto leaders to assist its approach to regulating the blockchain industry.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/cftc-forms-committee-regulating-blockchain-ai?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: SEC Chairman: Fully supports Congress clarifying the division of jurisdiction between the SEC and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission.
data.description: SEC Chairman Paul Atkins posted on the X platform that this week is significant for cryptocurrencies, and he fully supports Congress clarifying the jurisdictional division between the SEC and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission. The most important thing to do for investors right now is to bring the crypto asset market out of the regulatory gray area. He expects President Trump to sign a bipartisan market structure bill in the coming months, based on bipartisan legal provisions, to promote market innovation while protecting investor rights.

data.description:

Trump had signed a new executive order in August that made it easier for crypto and private equity to be included in 401(k) plans. “I write […]

data.title: U.S. SEC Chair Comments on Potential Seizure of Venezuela's Alleged Bitcoin Holdings
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on January 13, U.S. SEC Chair Paul Atkins stated that the decision on whether the United States will seize Venezuela's alleged Bitcoin assets remains uncertain. In an interview with Fox Business, Atkins addressed claims that Venezuela might possess up to $6 billion worth of Bitcoin, approximately 60,000 BTC. He noted that several blockchain analysts have been unable to verify these claims. The decision regarding the disposition of these assets will be made by other government departments, as the SEC is not involved in this matter.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Williams Affirms Feasibility of 2% Inflation Target
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Federal Reserve official Williams has stated that restoring the inflation rate to the 2% target is 'completely realistic.'

data.title: SEC Chairman: Whether the US will confiscate Venezuelan-held Bitcoin remains to be seen.
data.description: In an interview with Fox Business, U.S. SEC Chairman Paul Atkins stated that it remains to be seen whether U.S. authorities will confiscate the alleged Bitcoin held by Venezuela following the arrest of the Venezuelan president. Regarding reports that Venezuela holds $60 billion worth of Bitcoin (approximately 600,000 BTC), Atkins stated that Venezuela is not involved in such decisions and that they will be handled by other government departments. Several blockchain analytics firms have not yet confirmed the claim that the country holds $60 billion worth of cryptocurrency. Furthermore, the U.S. Senate Banking Committee plans to consider the Digital Asset Market Transparency Act (CLARITY) this Thursday. This bill aims to grant the Commodity Futures Trading Commission more regulatory power over digital assets, but may be delayed due to factors such as the 2026 midterm elections.

data.title: Bitmine increased its pledged holdings by 154,000 ETH today, bringing the total pledged ETH to $4.15 billion.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, Bitmine (@BitMNR) has staked an additional 154,208 ETH, worth $478.77 million. Currently, Bitmine has staked a total of 1,344,224 ETH, with a total value of $4.15 billion.
